articles.title: Scholars bar is closing after 15 years. A new concept is already planned.
articles.description:
The Downtown Crossing space is expected to reopen as a live music-focused restaurant and bar.

The post Scholars bar is closing after 15 years. A new concept is already planned. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Scholars bar in Downtown Crossing is closing after 15 years of operation.
A new live music-focused restaurant and bar is planned for the same location.
Local residents can expect a new entertainment venue to enhance the Downtown Crossing area.

articles.title: How much are you spending to attend the World Cup?
articles.description:
We want to hear from local, national, and international fans who are attending World Cup matches in Boston.

The post How much are you spending to attend the World Cup? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston residents are invited to share their World Cup spending experiences.
Local fans are expected to spend significantly on tickets, accommodations, and travel.
Increased tourism during the World Cup may boost Boston's economy in 2026.

articles.title: A first look at the massive outdoor bar coming to Cape Cod this summer
articles.description:
The space will feature a nearly 5,000 square-foot sail-covered main bar.

The post A first look at the massive outdoor bar coming to Cape Cod this summer appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Cape Cod will open a new outdoor bar this summer, enhancing local tourism.
The bar will feature a 5,000 square-foot sail-covered main area.
Boston residents can expect increased weekend trips to Cape Cod for leisure.

articles.title: Chelsea man pleads not guilty after allegedly threatening BU campus, students, deans
articles.description:
The 29-year-old is a student at BU, according to the university's student directory.

The post Chelsea man pleads not guilty after allegedly threatening BU campus, students, deans appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Chelsea man, 29, pleads not guilty to threatening Boston University campus.
Accused individual is a current student at BU, raising safety concerns.
Incident heightens anxiety among students and faculty regarding campus security.
